mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and increases the relative attractiveness of money-market instruments, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns with a rank correlation above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month payroll decline exceeding 50,000 workers signals imminent demand destruction and recession risk, triggering risk-off repositioning in equities and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in over 50% of instances when PAYEMS declines by more than 50 in a single month during any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high federal funds rates above 5% and rising unemployment rate above 4% trigger a stagflationary discount-rate and earnings-revision double hit, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during months when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% and UNRATE exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month retail sales growth above 0.5% signals robust consumer demand that translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when RSAFS grows by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1,300 thousand units signals construction sector contraction and backward linkages into durable goods demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in over 50% of rolling quarters when HOUST averages below 1,300 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production directly reduce aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors mark down earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Negative monthly INDPRO changes fail to predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns with a rank correlation above 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with restrictive federal funds rates squeeze real corporate cash flows and compress valuation multiples through higher real discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 55% of weeks when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment below 4% during late-cycle expansions signals an overheating labor market that forces aggressive monetary tightening and subsequent earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% during any 2-year sample.
